creator: itetcu@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, 5 Sep 2006 10:57:23 +0200
usleepless@gmail.com wrote:

> Dear Ion-Mihai,
> 
> On 9/5/06, Ion-Mihai IOnut Tetcu <itetcu@freebsd.org> wrote:
> > On Fri,  1 Sep 2006 23:25:42 +0200
> > "usleepless" <usleepless@gmail.com> wrote:
> >
> > >
> > > >Number:         102765
> > > >Category:       ports
> > > >Synopsis:       New port: multimedia/pvrxxx. A patched version of
> > > >pvr250 to support 150/500 cards.
> >
> > Can we just (optionally eventually) patch the existing port ?
> 
> we could. but i am not as far in autodetecting the cardtypes that this
> would work. furthermore, i have contacted the author of the original
> port and he is not very forthcoming. furthermore the pvr250-port is
> maintained by Edwin Groothuis, who has no inside knowledge of the
> code.
> 
> it is my intention to make this port work for all hauppauge pvr-style
> cards ( alla ivtv ). that's why i want it committed to the ports tree,
> and announce it on multimedia. at this moment, the driver works for 3
> people ( including me ).

OK, bug me next week if no one else takes this PR and I'll see what I
can do.

> 
> > > >Severity:       serious
> > > >Priority:       medium
> >
> > I see no reason for this PR to be "serious" and "medium", do you ?
> 
> no, me neither. first time i used pr-send. i assumed they would take
> sensible default values, sorry.

NP.

-- 
IOnut - Un^d^dregistered ;) FreeBSD "user"
  "Intellectual Property" is   nowhere near as valuable   as "Intellect"

BOFH excuse #349:
Stray Alpha Particles from memory packaging caused Hard Memory Error
on Server
